title: noronhia alleizettei
intro: Noronhia alleizettei, also known as the 'Bastardia' or 'Cemetery Tree', is a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae, native to Madagascar. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Noronhia alleizettei include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and various skin conditions, as well as its use as an anti-diarrheal agent. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into a tea or infusion, and the bark is also used to make a tincture.
